The classification of Nebelia angulata also includes the kingdom Chromista and the domain Eukaryota. Neptunia is not the only possible plant that might be confused with Nebelia, however, a plant with a similar name is the Nepeta, also known as the catmint. 

Here is a paragraph about Nepeta (Catmint):

Nepeta, commonly known as catmint, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The health benefits of Nepeta include its ability to act as an antispasmodic, reducing muscle cramps and spasms, as well as its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate pain and swelling. The bioactive constituents of Nepeta include nepetalactone, a monoterpene lactone that has been shown to have sedative and antiseizure effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Nepeta include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to calm anxiety, promote sleep, and soothe digestive issues.
